rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. KINK.COM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Plaintiffs Unruh Act Allegations
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 5, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT CYBERNET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Plaintiffs Unruh Act Allegations Defendant Cybernet Entertainment, LLC moves for summary adjudication of plaintiff's eleventh cause of action - Unruh Civil Rights Act (Civil Code §51 et seq.) - on six grounds. The motion is DENIED.Waiver by Conduct. Cybernet concedes "that no performer could participate in filming" of its "straight shoots" "without having first tested negative for STIs [sexually transmitted infections] and HIV," while the same was not true for its "gay shoots." Cybernet says "Plaintiff voluntarily chose to forego his right" to mandate HIV testing, but plaintiff says Cybernet never informed him of its "testing optional policy on gay shoots." (See PSSUMF 1, 17.) This creates an issue of material fact for trial. (See CCP §437c.) Motivation. Cybernet claims its conduct was not "substantially motivated by its perception of" plaintiff's "sexual orientation," citing "input" from the "gay community" for its policies and "cross over" by performers between gay and straight shoots. However, Cybernet's policies are facially discriminatory, and motivations are often implied, so a triable issue exists. (See Koebke v. Bernardo Heights Country Club (2005) 36 Cal.4th 824, 854.) Causation. Under the Unruh Act, injury is caused when a discriminatory policy is applied to the plaintiff, actual damage is not required. (Angelucci v. Century Supper Club (2007) 41 Cal.4th 160, 174.) Plaintiff need not have contracted HIV to have an Unruh Act claim. Statute of Limitations. As previously ruled on Cybernet's demurrer, plaintiff's Unruh Act claim relates back to the original complaint's filing. Workplace Discrimination. As also previously ruled on Cybernet's demurrer, the Unruh Act may apply to independent contractors such as plaintiff. (See Payne v. Anaheim Memorial Medical Center, Inc. (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 729, 747-48.) Written Waiver. Cybernet concedes that statutory damages are available to plaintiff, so its argument that plaintiff waived actual damages fails to dispose of the Unruh Act claim and summary adjudication is unavailable. (See DeCastro West Chodorow & Burns, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 410, 422.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff must prepare the proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: SEAN DEVRIES ET AL VS. SEAN KILLEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Atty'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 5, 2018, Line 1, Plaintiff'S Motion For Atty'S Fees Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: At issue is Plaintiffs' request for attorneys' fees against Defendants' prior counsel, Na'il Benjamin. On June 29, 2018, under CCP section 473(b), this Court granted Defendants' motion for relief from the imposition of evidentiary sanctions. Defendants' motion was premised on an error made by Mr. Benjamin, its prior counsel. The Court finds that Defendants are entitled to "reasonable compensatory legal fees" from Mr. Benjamin under CCP section 473(b) and awards them a combined total of $1,500. Fees to be paid to Plaintiffs by November 1, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID DIPUMA VS. ALFRED ANTHONY RAMOS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff David Dipuma'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Amended Complaint Adding Lyft, Inc. As A Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 5, 2018, Line 4, Plaintiff David Dipuma'S Motion To File Amended Complaint Adding Lyft, Inc. As A Defendant Plaintiff David Dipuma's motion to amend the complaint to add Lyft, Inc. as a defendant is GRANTED. Plaintiff says he recently learned in discovery that defendant Karl Duff was a Lyft driver en route to pick up passengers when a motor vehicle accident occurred. Duff argues that the same information could have been learned a year ago from a police report, but does not proffer a copy of the report. In any event, California's strong policy favors liberal amendment of pleadings. (Messler v. Bragg Management Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SHANNON RAGGIO VS. ST. MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Dignity Health'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S 2nd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 5, 2018, Line 12, Defendant Dignity Health'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S 2nd Amended Complaint Defendant Dignity Health's demurrers to the second amended complaint (SAC) are OVERRULED. Medical Negligence Claim. The SAC alleges that Dignity failed to obtain her consent before performing the medical procedure, contrary to its duties under 22 CRC §70707. (See SAC 14-15.) This suffices. Unlike in Moore v. Regents of University of California (1990) 51 Cal.3d 120, 133, Dignity's gatekeeping duty to obtain consent required no medical training. Battery. The SAC adequately alleges an offensive touching. (14-15, 29.) I cannot find whether a reasonable person would have approved the procedure at this stage of the case. Discovery may reveal which Dignity staff members did the alleged touching. Elder Abuse. The SAC adequately alleges that the battery was elder abuse. (See W&I Code §§15657, 15610.63.) Wrongful Death. Paragraph 37 of the SAC adequately alleges that Dignity's unconsented provision of anesthesia caused the death. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for plaintiff must prepare the proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: ROSS PSYHOGIOS VS. JEROME L. VITENSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Defendant Sharon Castle'S Further Responses Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 5, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ROSS PSYHOGIOS' Motion To Compel Defendant Sharon Castle'S Further Responses Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part; denied in part. The Court finds that Defendant's responses to the RFPDs are code compliant. A party need only state that "all responsive, non privileged, documents in its possession, care or control will be produced." Defendant has done that. The Court finds that the privilege log is sufficient as to the assertions of the attorney-client privilege, but insufficient as to any documents withheld relating to the subtenants. Each document withheld must be logged individually. It also appears that Defendant has withheld architectural drawings on the basis of copyright or trademark issues but has not provided evidence to sustain those objections. As such, the architectural drawings must be produced. As to the special interrogatories, the Court finds that Defendant's responses to Nos. 3, 4, 13 and 14 are insufficient. The motion as to the other requests/interrogatories is denied. Supplemental responses, documents, and the revised privilege log to be provided by October 26, 2018. All requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demands For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $4,560.00 Fr0om Defendant Sutter Valley Hospitals, Inc. Dba Sutter Tracy Community Hospital By Plaintiff Melissa Berry, By And Through Successors In Interest
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 5, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFFS MELISSA BERRY, KARIZMA BERRY, KHARMA BERRY, LOYAL TERRELL PRYOR'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demands For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $4,560.00 Fr0om Defendant Sutter Valley Hospitals, Inc. Dba Sutter Tracy Community Hospital By Plaintiff Melissa Berry, By And Through Successors In Interest Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required to discuss the following: The Court agrees with Plaintiff that Defendants' responses are not code compliant, i.e. CCP 2031.210 requires that the response not simply list the documents being produced but that "all responsive, non privileged documents, will be produced." Defendants responses do not say that. That being said, some of the requests at issue seem overbroad and vague. It is unclear to the Court whether Defendant has withheld any documents based on its overbroad objections. The parties should be prepared to address the specific requests in dispute in which there is a question about the scope of the information being requested. rulings.Case Title: EQBAL HUSSAIN VS. ANYUB ALI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 5, 2018, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT EQBAL HUSSAIN's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT Plaintiff Eqbal Hussain's CCP §425.16 motion to strike defendant Aiyub Ali's cross-complaint is GRANTED. To succeed on an anti-SLAPP motion, a moving party must first establish a prima facie case that the claim arose out of an act "in furtherance of the person's right of petition or free speech." (Id. at (b)(1).) The burden then shifts to the nonmoving party to establish a probability that he will prevail on his claim. (Id.) Hussain shows that his e-mail to Ali and other board members of the Fiji Muslim Sports Association was of public concern and thus protected speech. (See Wilbanks v. Wolk (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 883, 897.) Ali presents no evidence to support his claim of emotional distress and damage from Hussain's e-mail. Without such evidence, I cannot conclude there is a probability Ali will prevail on his cross-complaint. (See Wilson v. Parker, Covert & Chidester (2002) 28 Cal.4th 811, 821.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
